document.title: March 9, 2023 Meeting of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- Combined FDA and Genentech Briefing Document

key_concerns: Potential underestimation of myelosuppression due to lack of midcycle labs
Inadequate PRO tolerability assessment per FDA
key_positives: Consistent benefit across secondary endpoints (EFSeff, DFS, DOR)
Comparable overall safety profile to R-CHOP
High relative dose intensity indicating tolerability

key_concerns: Potential underestimation of myelosuppression due to lack of midcycle labs
Inadequate PRO tolerability assessment per FDA
key_positives: Consistent benefit across secondary endpoints (EFSeff, DFS, DOR)
Comparable overall safety profile to R-CHOP
High relative dose intensity indicating tolerability
evidence_notes: PFS benefit 'both statistically significant and clinically meaningful' per Applicant but FDA notes 'effect size was modest'
Safety profile 'comparable to R-CHOP' though Table 12 shows higher febrile neutropenia (14.3% vs 8.0%)
FDA: 'lack of superiority is not suitable evidence for claims of comparability' regarding PROs
MYELOSUPPRESSION: 'midcycle nadir labs were not collected' raising FDA concern of underestimation
OS 'consistently shown HR<1' but 'remains immature and was not statistically different'

signal.label: POSITIVE
rationale: Concerns: Modest PFS benefit (1-year difference 4.1%, 2-year 6.5%); No overall survival improvement; Increased febrile neutropenia and infections; Potential underestimation of myelosuppression due to lack of midcycle labs; Inadequate PRO tolerability assessment per FDA

Positives: Statistically significant PFS improvement (HR 0.73); Consistent benefit across secondary endpoints (EFSeff, DFS, DOR); Comparable overall safety profile to R-CHOP; High relative dose intensity indicating tolerability; Addresses unmet need in 1L DLBCL
